system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   0.001;

vertices
(

    ( -50  25  25 )   // 0
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 1
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 3

    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 6
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    (  25   0  25 )   // 8
    (  25   0 -25 )   // 9
    (  25 -50 -25 )   // 10
    (  25 -50  25 )   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight duct
    (  25  -75  25 )   // 12
    (  25  -75 -25 )   // 13
    (  25 -125 -25 )   // 14
    (  25 -125  25 )   // 15

    ( -500  -75  25 )   // 16
    ( -500  -75 -25 )   // 17
    ( -500 -125 -25 )   // 18
    ( -500 -125  25 )   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1a: Transition from bend to outlet
    hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section
    hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 top
            (0 4 5 1)
            // Block 1 top
            (7 8 9 6)
            // Block 1a top
            (11 12 13 10)
            // Block 2 top
            (12 16 17 13)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 bottom
            (3 7 6 2)
            // Block 1 bottom
            (3 11 10 2)
            // Block 1a bottom
            (3 15 14 2)
            // Block 2 bottom
            (15 19 18 14)
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {

        faces
        (

            (0 3 7 4)
            // Block 1 front
            (7 3 11 8)
            // Block 1a front
            (11 3 15 12)
            // Block 2 front
            (12 15 19 16)
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 back
            (1 5 6 2)
            // Block 1 back
            (6 5 9 8)
            // Block 1a back
            (2 6 14 10)
            // Block 2 back
            (14 10 17 13)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_Bmodel/runs/squareBend/3/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 46.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block is "inside-out"

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) ... is inside-out
```

This means the vertex ordering in the first hex block (and potentially others) does not follow the right-hand rule required by OpenFOAM. When you define a hex block with vertices `(v0 v1 v2 v3 v4 v5 v6 v7)`, the first four vertices should form a face with a normal pointing **into** the block, and the second four should form the opposite face.

### Root Cause

Looking at the vertex definitions:
- Vertex 0: (-50, 25, 25)
- Vertex 1: (-50, 25, -25)
- Vertex 2: (-50, -25, -25)
- Vertex 3: (-50, -25, 25)

The face `(0 4 5 1)` uses vertices 0→(-50,25,25), 4→(0,25,25), 5→(0,25,-25), 1→(-50,25,-25). This face lies at y=+25. For a hex block, the face normal should point from the first face toward the second face. But the issue is more fundamental — the vertex ordering needs to be checked carefully against the geometry.

However, the real problem is likely the **vertex ordering within each hex block definition**. Let me examine the blocks:

#### Block 0: `hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)`
Face 1: `(0 4 5 1)` — these are at x=-50 to x=0, y=+25
Face 2: `(3 7 6 2)` — these are at x=-50 to x=0, y=-25

For Block 0, if we want the block to extend in the **+y direction** (from y=-25 to y=+25), then Face 1 should be the **-y face** (back) and Face 2 should be the **+y face** (front). Looking at the normals:
- Back face `(0 4 5 1)`: points in -y direction ✓
- Front face `(3 7 6 2)`: points in +y direction ✓

So Block 0's internal ordering seems fine.

#### Block 1: `hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)`

Let me trace this through. Vertices involved:
- 7: (0, -25, 25), 8: (25, 0, 25), 9: (25, 0, -25), 6: (0, -25, -25)
- 3: (-50, -25, 25), 11: (25, -50, 25), 10: (25, -50, -25), 2: (-50, -25, -25)

This block represents the bend transition. The issue may be that the vertex ordering doesn't maintain consistent winding across shared faces with adjacent blocks. Specifically, the shared face between Block 0 and Block 1 is `(2 6 7 3)` vs `(7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)` — they share vertices 2, 3, 6, 7 but the ordering differs.

**Recommendation:** Redefine the hex blocks so that all shared internal faces have matching vertex orderings. A reliable approach is to ensure that for every hex block, when looking at the two hexagonal faces, the normal of the first face points outward/inward consistently relative to the block volume. Consider reordering Block 1 as `hex (2 6 7 3 0 4 5 1)` or verify the current ordering against the actual geometry. Similarly check Blocks 1a and 2.

A practical fix: **reverse the vertex ordering** in all hex block definitions until the block is no longer inside-out. Start with Block 0: change `(0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` to `(0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)`. Then check Block 1: change `(7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)` to `(2 3 11 10 6 7 8 9)`, etc.

---

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points" file

This is a cascading error — since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh was generated, so `rhoSimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files. Once `blockMesh` runs successfully, this will be resolved automatically.

---

## Additional Issue: Boundary Name Mismatches

After fixing the mesh, there will be a mismatch between boundary patches defined in `blockMeshDict` and those referenced in the `0/` field files. The `blockMeshDict` defines patches: `inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`. But the field files (`0/T`, `0/U`, `0/k`, `0/e`, `0/nut`) reference patches named `Default_Boundary_Region`, `innerWall`, `outerWall`, and `walls`.

**Recommendation:** Update all field files in `0/` to use the exact patch names from `blockMeshDict`: `inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`. Remove references to `Default_Boundary_Region`, `innerWall`, `outerWall`, and `frontAndBack` (which doesn't exist in the mesh).

Specifically:
- **`0/T`**: Replace `Default_Boundary_Region` with separate entries for `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, all using `zeroGradient`.
- **`0/U`**: Replace `frontAndBack` with separate `leftWall` and `rightWall` entries using `noSlip`.
- **`0/k`**, **`0/e`**, **`0/nut`**: Replace `innerWall`/`outerWall` with separate entries for `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall` using `kqRWallFunction`/`epsilonWallFunction`/`calculated` as appropriate.
- **`0/alphat`**: Already correctly references `Default_Boundary_Region` — either rename that patch in `blockMeshDict` to `Default_Boundary_Region` (not recommended) or replace it with individual wall entries like the others.
- **`0/p`**: Already correctly references the wall patches — just remove the `innerWall`/`outerWall` entries and add `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall` with `zeroGradient`.

---

## Summary of Actions

1. **Fix hex block vertex ordering** in `blockMeshDict` until no blocks are inside-out (use `blockMesh -verbose` to debug).
2. **Align all `0/` field files** with the boundary patch names from `blockMeshDict` (`inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`).</reviewer_analysis>

Change Block 1a hex definition from hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2) to hex (5 10 11 6 2 15 14 13) aligning with Block 1 vertex pattern; Change Block 2 hex definition from hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) to hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19) to fix potential inside-out issue'}, {'file': '0/T', 'changes': 'Remove Default_Boundary_Region entry; <user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
